summary:
Sotatercept effectively reduces pulmonary vascular resistance and improves exercise capacity in PAH patients, demonstrating clinically meaningful benefit despite a single reported fatality related to high-dose administration. This supports its use in managing PAH; therefore, it is deemed effective.\boxed{A}

The provided information indicates that sotatercept significantly lowers pulmonary vascular resistance and enhances functional status through increased six-minute walking distances in PAH patients—key markers indicating therapeutic effect—and although a rare severe adverse event occurred during treatment, no major contraindications are established against its application. Hence, considering both efficacy and risk profile within available literature, sotatercept can be regarded as an effective therapy for PAH.\boxed{A}
